calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Report Of Status With A Petition Requesting Additional Time For Estate Administration Must Be Filed At Least Two Weeks Before That Status Date. Appearance Will Be Required On Nov-23-2026 If No Petition Has Been Filed.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
Off Calendar May-11-2026 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Feb-09-2026 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-06-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. No one appeared to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The will dated July 8, 2002 is admitted to probate. John Fehrn is appointed as the Administrator of the estate with will annexed with full authority. Bond is set at $1,108,000. Order for the same signed in open Court. A status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for February 9, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. If either petition is filed two weeks prior, no appearance is required on that date. Isidoor Bornstein is appointed probate referee.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-16-2024 Continued To Jan-06-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Melanie Emmons for John Fehrn, Petitioner (via Zoom).

The court noted that recent filings in the matter had not yet been reviewed. The court proposed a short continuance to allow the examiner time to review the amended petition and address pending issues. Counsel agreed with the court's proposal and confirmed their availability. The court continued the matter to January 6, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-16-2024 Continued To Dec-16-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Melanie Emmons appeared by zoom for John Fehrn (petitioner).

Mrs. Emmons stated that they found a holographic will in the safe deposit box, that she hoped to file everything, but she was unable to contact one of the witnesses and asked the court to extend the letters of special administration. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to December 16, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The letters of special administration are extended December 16, 2024. Mrs. Emmons is to provide a form of order to the probate examiner.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: E. Melendez. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-15-2024 Continued To Sep-16-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Fasesky appeared by zoom for John Fehrn (special administrator).

The court stated that there are a lot of examiner's notes that have not been addressed. Mr. Fasesky stated that as of today Mr. Ferhn has received delay and resistance from Bank of America ("B of A") with respect to allowing him access to the safe deposit box to search for a will, that he sent a letter to B of A, but they have not responded, and requested a 60-day extension of special administration. Mr. Fasesky also requested that number three of the order for special administration be amended to order the West Portal branch of Bank of America to immediately allow Mr. Ferhn access to the safe deposit box which will include drilling of the box if needed. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to September 16, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Special administration of the estate is also extended to September 16, 2024. Mr. Fasesky is to provide a clean order to the probate examiner. If something else is needed with respect to the safe deposit box, Mr. Fasesky is to do a drop off ex parte petition.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Tan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-22-2024 Continued To Jul-15-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Fasesky appeared by zoom for John Fehrn (special administrator).

The court noted that there is a substantial list of open issues that need to be addressed. Mr. Fasesky stated that this is mainly because the heirs of the decedent are in Norway and requested that the special letters be extended and that the special administrator be authorized to open an estate bank account. Mr. Fasesky also stated that the order extending the special letters has been submitted electronically and sent to the Probate Examiner. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to July 15, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court will sign the order extending the letters.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-29-2024 Granted Per Written Order
